001/*

003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
021
022import java.io.*;
023
024import org.apache.juneau.commons.io.*;
025
026/**
027 * Similar to a {@link PushbackReader} with a pushback buffer of 1 character.
028 *
029 * <p>
030 * Code is optimized to work with a 1 character buffer.
031 *
032 * <p>
033 * Additionally keeps track of current line and column number, and provides the ability to set mark points and capture
034 * characters from the previous mark point.
035 *
036 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
037 *    <li class='warn'>This class is not thread safe.
038 * </ul>
039 *
040 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
041 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/SerializersAndParsers">Serializers and Parsers</a>
042
043 * </ul>
044 */
045@SuppressWarnings({
046   "resource", // ParserReader is managed by caller
047   "java:S115" // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention (e.g., MSG_bufferUnderflow)
048})
049public class ParserReader extends Reader implements Positionable {
050
051   // Error message constants
052   private static final String MSG_bufferUnderflow = "Buffer underflow.";
053
054   /**
055    * Maximum number of previously-read characters preserved as unread lookback when the buffer is
056    * refilled <em>without</em> an active {@link #mark()}. Originally 10, which was sufficient for
057    * JSON's short tokens but too small for YAML's indent-aware parser: a deeply-nested OpenAPI 3.1
058    * document can have indent depth well past 10 chars on a single line, and the YAML parser's
059    * {@code skipBlanksAndCountIndent} / {@code unreadSpaces} pair (and similar peek-then-unread
060    * patterns in block-scalar handling) tries to unread the entire indent at once. When the indent
061    * read crossed the buffer boundary, the rest underflowed. 256 chars covers any realistic YAML
062    * document indent depth (128 levels at 2-space indent) while still leaving 768 chars per
063    * 1024-char refill for forward-progress reads.
064    */
065   private static final int UNMARKED_LOOKBACK_CHARS = 256;
066
067   /** Wrapped reader */
068   protected final Reader r;
069
070   private char[] buff;       // Internal character buffer
071   private int line = 1;      // Current line number
072   private int column;        // Current column number
073   private int iCurrent;      // Current pointer into character buffer
074   private int iMark = -1;    // Mark position in buffer
075   private int iEnd;          // The last good character position in the buffer
076   private boolean endReached;
077   private boolean holesExist;
078   private final boolean unbuffered;
079
080   /**
081    * Constructor.
082    *
083    * @param pipe The parser input.
084    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
085    */
086   public ParserReader(ParserPipe pipe) throws IOException {
087      this.unbuffered = pipe.unbuffered;
088      if (pipe.isString()) {
089         String in = pipe.getInputAsString();
090         this.r = new CharSequenceReader(in);
091         this.buff = new char[in.length() < 1024 ? in.length() : 1024];
092      } else {
093         Reader reader = pipe.getReader();
094         if (reader instanceof ParserReader reader2)
095            this.r = reader2.r;
096         else
097            this.r = reader;
098         this.buff = new char[1024];
099      }
100      pipe.setPositionable(this);
101   }
102
103   /**
104    * No-op.
105    *
106    * <p>
107    * Input readers are closed in the {@link ParserPipe} class.
108    *
109    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred trying to read from the reader.
110    */
111   @Override /* Overridden from Reader */
112   public void close() throws IOException {
113      // No-op
114   }
115
116   /**
117    * Trims off the last character in the marking buffer.
118    *
119    * <p>
120    * Useful for removing escape characters from sequences.
121    *
122    * @return This object.
123    */
124   public final ParserReader delete() {
125      return delete(1);
126   }
127
128   /**
129    * Trims off the specified number of last characters in the marking buffer.
130    * Useful for removing escape characters from sequences.
131    *
132    * @param count The number of characters to delete.
133    * @return This object.
134    */
135   public final ParserReader delete(int count) {
136      for (var i = 0; i < count; i++)
137         buff[iCurrent - i - 1] = 127;
138      holesExist = true;
139      return this;
140   }
141
142   /**
143    * Returns the contents of the reusable character buffer as a string, and resets the buffer for next usage.
144    *
145    * @return The contents of the reusable character buffer as a string.
146    */
147   public final String getMarked() { return getMarked(0, 0); }
148
149   /**
150    * Same as {@link #getMarked()} except allows you to specify offsets into the buffer.
151    *
152    * <p>
153    * For example, to return the marked string, but trim the first and last characters, call the following:
154    * <p class='bjava'>
155    *    getFromMarked(1, -1);
156    * </p>
157    *
158    * @param offsetStart The offset of the start position.
159    * @param offsetEnd The offset of the end position.
160    * @return The contents of the reusable character buffer as a string.
161    */
162   public final String getMarked(int offsetStart, int offsetEnd) {
163      int offset = 0;
164
165      // Holes are \u00FF 'delete' characters that we need to get rid of now.
166      if (holesExist) {
167         for (var i = iMark; i < iCurrent; i++) {
168            char c = buff[i];
169            if (c == 127)
170               offset++;
171            else
172               buff[i - offset] = c;
173         }
174         holesExist = false;
175      }
176      int start = iMark + offsetStart;
177      int len = iCurrent - iMark + offsetEnd - offsetStart - offset;
178      var s = new String(buff, start, len);
179      iMark = -1;
180      return s;
181   }
182
183   @Override /* Overridden from Positionable */
184   public Position getPosition() { return new Position(line, column); }
185
186   /**
187    * Start buffering the calls to read() so that the text can be gathered from the mark point on calling {@code getFromMarked()}.
188    */
189   public final void mark() {
190      iMark = iCurrent;
191   }
192
193   /**
194    * Reads a numeric string from the specified reader.
195    *
196    * @return The parsed number string.
197    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
198    */
199   public String parseNumberString() throws IOException {
200      mark();
201      int c = 0;
202      while (true) {
203         c = read();
204         if (c == -1 || ! isNumberChar((char)c)) {
205            if (c != -1)
206               unread();
207            break;
208         }
209      }
210      return getMarked();
211   }
212
213   /**
214    * Peeks the next character in the stream.
215    *
216    * <p>
217    * This is equivalent to doing a {@code read()} followed by an {@code unread()}.
218    *
219    * @return The peeked character, or (char)-1 if the end of the stream has been reached.
220    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred trying to read from the reader.
221    */
222   public final int peek() throws IOException {
223      int c = read();
224      if (c != -1)
225         unread();
226      return c;
227   }
228
229   /**
230    * Same as {@link #peek()} but skips over any whitespace characters.
231    *
232    * <p>
233    * This is equivalent to doing a {@code read()} followed by an {@code unread()}.
234    *
235    * @return The peeked character, or (char)-1 if the end of the stream has been reached.
236    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred trying to read from the reader.
237    */
238   public final int peekSkipWs() throws IOException {
239      while (true) {
240         var c = read();
241         var isWs = Character.isWhitespace(c);
242         if (c != -1 && ! isWs)
243            unread();
244         if (! isWs)
245            return c;
246      }
247   }
248
249   /**
250    * Reads a single character.
251    *
252    * <p>
253    * Note that this method does NOT process extended unicode characters (i.e. characters above 0x10000), but rather
254    * returns them as two <jk>char</jk>s.
255    * Use {@link #readCodePoint()} to ensure proper handling of extended unicode.
256    *
257    * @return The character read, or -1 if the end of the stream has been reached.
258    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred trying to read from the reader.
259    */
260   @Override /* Overridden from Reader */
261   public final int read() throws IOException {
262      int c = readFromBuff();
263      if (c == -1)
264         return -1;
265      if (c == '\n') {
266         line++;
267         column = 0;
268      } else {
269         column++;
270      }
271      return c;
272   }
273
274   /**
275    * Subclasses can override this method to provide additional filtering.
276    *
277    * <p>
278    * Default implementation simply calls the same method on the underlying reader.
279    */
280   @Override /* Overridden from Reader */
281   public int read(char[] cbuf, int off, int len) throws IOException {
282      return unbuffered ? r.read(cbuf, off, 1) : r.read(cbuf, off, len);
283   }
284
285   /**
286    * Read the specified number of characters off the stream.
287    *
288    * @param num The number of characters to read.
289    * @return The characters packaged as a String.
290    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred trying to read from the reader.
291    */
292   public final String read(int num) throws IOException {
293      var c = new char[num];
294      for (var i = 0; i < num; i++) {
295         var c2 = read();
296         if (c2 == -1)
297            return new String(c, 0, i);
298         c[i] = (char)c2;
299      }
300      return new String(c);
301   }
302
303   /**
304    * Same as {@link #read()} but detects and combines extended unicode characters (characters above 0x10000).
305    *
306    * @return The character read, or -1 if the end of the stream has been reached.
307    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred trying to read from the reader.
308    */
309   public final int readCodePoint() throws IOException {
310      int c = read();
311
312      // Characters that take up 2 chars.
313      if (c >= 0xd800 && c <= 0xdbff) {
314         var low = read();
315         if (low >= 0xdc00 && low <= 0xdfff)
316            c = 0x10000 + ((c - 0xd800) << 10) + (low - 0xdc00);
317      }
318
319      return c;
320   }
321
322   /**
323    * Same as {@link #read()} but skips over any whitespace characters.
324    *
325    * @return The first non-whitespace character, or -1 if the end of stream reached.
326    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
327    */
328   public final int readSkipWs() throws IOException {
329      while (true) {
330         var c = read();
331         if (c == -1 || ! Character.isWhitespace(c))
332            return c;
333      }
334   }
335
336   /**
337    * Replace the last read character in the buffer with the specified character.
338    *
339    * @param c The new character.
340    * @return This object.
341    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
342    */
343   public final ParserReader replace(char c) throws IOException {
344      return replace(c, 1);
345   }
346
347   /**
348    * Replaces the last character in the marking buffer with the specified character.
349    *
350    * <p>
351    * <c>offset</c> must be at least <c>1</c> for normal characters, and <c>2</c> for extended
352    * unicode characters in order for the replacement to fit into the buffer.
353    *
354    * @param c The new character.
355    * @param offset The offset.
356    * @return This object.
357    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
358    */
359   public final ParserReader replace(int c, int offset) throws IOException {
360      if (c < 0x10000) {
361         if (offset < 1)
362            throw ioex(MSG_bufferUnderflow);
363         buff[iCurrent - offset] = (char)c;
364      } else {
365         if (offset < 2)
366            throw ioex(MSG_bufferUnderflow);
367         c -= 0x10000;
368         buff[iCurrent - offset] = (char)(0xd800 + (c >> 10));
369         buff[iCurrent - offset + 1] = (char)(0xdc00 + (c & 0x3ff));
370         offset--;
371      }
372      // Fill in the gap with DEL characters.
373      for (var i = 1; i < offset; i++)
374         buff[iCurrent - i] = 127;
375      holesExist |= (offset > 1);
376      return this;
377   }
378
379   /**
380    * Pushes the last read character back into the stream.
381    *
382    * @return This object.
383    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred trying to read from the reader.
384    */
385   public ParserReader unread() throws IOException {
386      if (iCurrent <= 0)
387         throw ioex(MSG_bufferUnderflow);
388      iCurrent--;
389      if (column == 0)
390         line--;
391      else
392         column--;
393      return this;
394   }
395
396   @SuppressWarnings({
397      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for buffer reading logic
398   })
399   private final int readFromBuff() throws IOException {
400      while (iCurrent >= iEnd) {
401         if (endReached)
402            return -1;
403
404         // If there's still space at the end of this buffer, fill it.
405         // Make sure there's at least 2 character spaces free for extended unicode characters.
406         if (iEnd + 1 < buff.length) {
407            int x = read(buff, iCurrent, buff.length - iEnd);
408            if (x == -1) {
409               endReached = true;
410               return -1;
411            }
412            iEnd += x;
413
414         } else {
415            // If we're currently marking, then we want to copy from the current mark point
416            // to the beginning of the buffer and then fill in the remainder of buffer.
417            if (iMark >= 0) {
418
419               // If we're marking from the beginning of the array, we double the size of the
420               // buffer.  This isn't likely to occur often.
421               if (iMark == 0) {
422                  var buff2 = new char[buff.length << 1];
423                  System.arraycopy(buff, 0, buff2, 0, buff.length);
424                  buff = buff2;
425
426                  // Otherwise, we copy what's currently marked to the beginning of the buffer.
427               } else {
428                  int copyBuff = iMark;
429                  System.arraycopy(buff, copyBuff, buff, 0, buff.length - copyBuff);
430                  iCurrent -= copyBuff;
431                  iMark -= copyBuff;
432               }
433               int expected = buff.length - iCurrent;
434
435               int x = read(buff, iCurrent, expected);
436               if (x == -1) {
437                  endReached = true;
438                  iEnd = iCurrent;
439                  return -1;
440               }
441               iEnd = iCurrent + x;
442            } else {
443               // Copy the last UNMARKED_LOOKBACK_CHARS chars in the buffer to the beginning so
444               // callers can still unread() that far across a buffer-boundary refill. See the
445               // constant's javadoc for why 256 (not 10) — widened to handle YAML's deep-indent
446               // peek-then-unread patterns where a single line's indent can exceed 10 chars.
447               int copyBuff = Math.min(iCurrent, UNMARKED_LOOKBACK_CHARS);
448               System.arraycopy(buff, iCurrent - copyBuff, buff, 0, copyBuff);
449
450               // Number of characters we expect to copy on the next read.
451               int expected = buff.length - copyBuff;
452               int x = read(buff, copyBuff, expected);
453               iCurrent = copyBuff;
454               if (x == -1) {
455                  endReached = true;
456                  iEnd = iCurrent;
457                  return -1;
458               }
459               iEnd = iCurrent + x;
460            }
461         }
462      }
463      return buff[iCurrent++];
464   }
465}
